step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{A} Based on the retrieved medical evidence, interferons are described as key players in defending against viral infections, activating immune cells like natural killer cells and macrophages, enhancing antigen presentation via MHC molecules, and contributing significantly to antiviral activities throughout the viral life cycle. Their actions—such as stimulating the expression of antiviral genes, inducing apoptosis inhibition in infected cells only when needed, and modulating immune responses—are explicitly tied to defense mechanisms. Thus, interferons function definitively as defensive proteins central to immune surveillance and protection.
